name: dotnetclr
explanation: .NET CLR metrics are collected via Windows Performance Counters (PDH) on the local host where the Agent runs. The spec uses `template: instances/perf_counters` and the check inherits `PerfCountersBaseCheckWithLegacySupport`. There is no remote host/port in the default mode; the check reads the local `.NET CLR Memory`, `.NET CLR Exceptions`, etc. perf-counter object set. Auto-config reduces to detecting whether any .NET CLR-instrumented process is registered with PDH on the Windows host and enabling the check with the default counter list. The hidden `pdh_legacy` template can target a remote machine via username/password but is not the default path.
notes: Pattern H (local Windows host detection), same shape as aspdotnet and iis. Auto-config trigger is '.NET CLR perf-counter objects present on this machine', not a network probe.
